subject: English
chapter: Poem – The Tale of Custard the Dragon

activities_projects.title: Character Trait Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the personality traits of each character.
steps: Read the poem carefully.
List the characters: Belinda, Custard, Mustard, Ink, Blink, and the pirate.
Write 3–4 sentences describing each character’s personality using lines from the poem.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand characters and support ideas with textual evidence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of character description and supporting lines.

activities_projects.title: Theme of True Courage
activities_projects.objective: Understand the theme of real bravery in the poem.
steps: Identify how the other characters behave before the pirate arrives.
Observe how they react when the pirate appears.
Write a short paragraph explaining why Custard shows true courage.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze how actions reveal true character.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of explanation and understanding of theme.

activities_projects.title: Rhythm and Rhyme Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine rhyme scheme and rhythm in the poem.
steps: Choose two stanzas from the poem.
Identify the rhyming pattern.
Explain how rhyme and rhythm make the poem lively and fun.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize poetic sound patterns and their effect.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of rhyme pattern and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Humor in the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Explore how the poet creates humor.
steps: Find 4–5 humorous descriptions in the poem.
Explain why they are funny.
Discuss how humor makes the story enjoyable.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate the humorous tone in poetry.

activities_projects.title: Imagery Illustration
activities_projects.objective: Understand imagery used in the poem.
steps: Choose a stanza with vivid description.
Draw the scene described in the stanza.
Write 2–3 lines explaining how the poet creates the image.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and correct interpretation of imagery.

activities_projects.title: Role Play with Dialogue
steps: Form a group and assign characters.
Recreate the scene when the pirate enters.
Add simple dialogues to show each character’s reaction.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and accurate portrayal of character reactions.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Context Activity
steps: Find 6–7 challenging words from the poem.
Write their meanings and synonyms.
Use each word in a meaningful sentence.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen vocabulary and contextual usage.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and proper sentence formation.

activities_projects.title: Event Sequencing
activities_projects.objective: Understand the flow of events in the poem.
steps: List the major events of the poem.
Arrange them in the correct order.
Explain which event is the turning point.

activities_projects.title: Comparing Expectations and Reality
activities_projects.objective: Analyze contrast between appearance and reality.
steps: Describe how the other characters think about Custard.
Explain what actually happens when the pirate appears.
Write a paragraph on how the poet shows that appearances can be misleading.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze deeper meaning of the poem.
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation and connection to text.

activities_projects.title: Paragraph on the Poem’s Message
activities_projects.objective: Understand the message conveyed by the poet.
steps: Think about what the poet wants readers to learn.
Write a paragraph (6–7 sentences) explaining the poem’s message about courage and friendship.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret and express the central idea of the poem.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, relevance, and depth of explanation.
